principle.name: Source-first interpretation
principle.description: Treat documents under business-blueprints as the business source of truth for generation tasks in this mode.
principle.rationale: The quality of generated business objects depends on preserving the business meaning found in source documents before translating them into JSON.

principle.name: Repository-aligned generation
principle.description: Reuse existing repository conventions, helpers, and known artifact shapes whenever possible.
principle.rationale: This prevents incompatible output structures and keeps the mode aligned with downstream toolkit packaging workflows.

principle.name: Normalization before emission
principle.description: Build an internal structured understanding of business objects before generating files.
principle.rationale: Direct file generation from unstructured text increases duplication, inconsistency, and type mismatches.

guideline.rule: Use user-provided business context to group outputs in the standard business-objects structure
guideline.good_example: Generate related business objects into business-objects/generated/[context]/ with matching entries in business-objects/catalog/[context].catalog.json
guideline.bad_example: Scatter generated objects across unrelated directories

guideline.good_example: Use singular PascalCase names for business object files when repository examples support that convention
guideline.bad_example: Mix plural, lowercase, and ad hoc file names for similar object types

guideline.rule: Keep generated catalogs deterministic and aligned with the fixed catalog locations
guideline.good_example: Use a predictable ordering of generated artifacts inside business-objects/catalog/[context].catalog.json and business-objects/catalog/master.catalog.json
guideline.bad_example: Emit catalogs with unstable ordering or inconsistent references

guideline.rule: Separate entities, value lists, and references explicitly
guideline.rationale: Documents often describe reusable values, embedded structures, and linked entities in the same paragraph. These should not automatically become the same kind of artifact.

guideline.rule: Capture ambiguity instead of hiding it
guideline.rationale: If a field type, required status, or relationship is unclear, record the assumption or surface the ambiguity rather than silently inventing a model.

guideline.rule: Prefer consistency over overly clever inference
guideline.rationale: Repeated, deterministic mappings are more valuable than speculative model generation that may be difficult to package or maintain later.

boundary.rule: Do not package generated business object artifacts in this mode
boundary.reason: Packaging belongs to the dedicated packaging workflow

boundary.rule: Do not deploy generated artifacts to BAW servers in this mode
boundary.reason: Deployment belongs to packaging and deployment workflows

boundary.rule: Do not create unrelated widget or coach implementations unless explicitly requested in another mode
boundary.reason: This mode is dedicated to document-to-business-object generation work

category.name: before_generation
category.item: Read the blueprint sources and relevant existing repository patterns
Identify the intended business context and grouping
Determine whether repository helpers already imply the target JSON shape

category.name: during_generation
category.item: Normalize names, fields, and references before writing artifacts
Write context and master catalogs under business-objects/catalog/
Write parse reports under business-objects/reports/ when needed
Record assumptions when document meaning is incomplete

category.name: before_completion
category.item: Confirm generated outputs align with repository conventions
Confirm catalogs are suitable for later packaging workflows
Keep packaging and deployment work out of scope
